I treated the 73 prior turns as external proposals, not as proof. The prompt fixes the special tame-format presentation: the first two generators are distinguished tame generators ¤â,¤ä, the tame relation ¤ä¤â=¤ä2 is implicit, later generators lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are represented modulo M=85667662080.The only still-unrefuted candidate in the prior log remains the dynamic tame-inertia word.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ¤Ç=¤Ç2ÔłłZ and ╬Ě=2¤Ç on odd tame inertia,r=x¤âÔłĺ¤Çx¤â¤ÇxÔłĺ1(x¤äÔłĺ╬Ě)¤Ç ((y¤ä)¤Ç)¤âyÔłĺ1,where ¤ÇÔëí40491355905,Ôłĺ¤ÇÔëí45176306175,Ôłĺ╬ĚÔëí50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of Ôłĺ╬Ě is immaterial here, since that exponent is applied only to ¤ä, whose finite tame images have odd order.The stable algebra from the late prior turns remains right when stated as equations. For a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character ¤äÔćŽ╬▒ţÇá=1, with t the deck involution in the sign double cover, the Fox coefficient forNa (u)=(u¤äa)¤ÇisDa=j=0ÔłĹ¤ÇÔłĺ1(t╬▒a)j,and it is characterized by (1Ôłĺt╬▒a)Da=1Ôłĺ(t╬▒a)¤Ç=1Ôłĺt.ThusDa=(1Ôłĺt)(1Ôłĺt╬▒a)Ôłĺ1.

This is the direction that makes Da radical and Ôłĺ1+DÔłĺ╬Ě a unit. Likewise, in the trivial tame-inertia block the elimination polynomial must be defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+TÔłĺs,sÔëí¤Ç(modf),up to the harmless convention TÔćöTÔłĺ1. This is the safe form; one must not invert 1+T inside F2 [T]/(TfÔłĺ1).New Turn 74 correction: the Turn 73 square-coordinate argument used the wrong class

Turn 73 claimed that the two universal first-IA square coordinates b[2]and S[2]vanish arithmetically because the two trivial Kummer classes are [Ôłĺ1] and [¤ľ], both fixed in L├Ś/L├Ś4. The S-part is plausible after the standard marking S=¤âf Ôćö¤ľ, but the b-part is not justified as written.Here is the issue. In the candidateÔÇÖs finite-tame abelianization, the unique C2 -torsion factor does not come from b. It comes from the tame-inertia-invariant regular X-norm. In the notation of the prior reductions, the ╬╗-part of the abelianized relation leaves a single torsion relation2NX=0,NX=regular┬áX-orbitÔłĹXg.

The b- and S-directions are free Z2 -directions.By local class field theory, the torsion subgroup of the pro-2 completion of L├Śfor a tame 2-adic extension L is ╬╝2={┬▒1}, since no tame extension of Q2 contains i. Therefore the reciprocity-side element Ôłĺ1 must correspond to the candidateÔÇÖs C2 -torsion line, i.e. the regular norm NX, not to the free b-line.

This matters because a b[2]-component is a mod-4 change in a free b-coordinate. It is detected by an order-4 Kummer lift dual to b. The class [Ôłĺ1]ÔłłL├Ś/L├Ś4 has order 2, not order 4, and it detects the torsion line, not the free b-square direction. So Turn 73ÔÇÖs conclusion¤Áb=0 does not follow from the fixedness of [Ôłĺ1].The corrected base-field interpretation is likely this: in the base GQ2 (2) specialization, the torsion generator corresponds to Ôłĺ1, the marked unramified generator corresponds to 2, and the remaining free unit direction corresponds to the standard class 5ÔłłQ2├Ś/Q2├Ś4. Thus the correct replacement for Turn 73ÔÇÖs sentence should be:bÔłĘshould┬ábe┬ánormalized┬áto┬áthe┬áorder-4┬áclass┬á[5],SÔłĘto┬á[¤ľ],not bÔłĘ=[Ôłĺ1].With that normalization, the intended square-coordinate vanishing becomes plausible again: 5ÔłłQ2├Śis fixed by all tame automorphisms, and ¤ä(¤ľ)=╬Âe ¤ľ differs from ¤ľ by an odd root of unity, which is a fourth power in ╬╝e. Hence [5] and [¤ľ] are fixed in L├Ś/L├Ś4. But this is an additional Kummer-basis identification that the prior log has not proved uniformly.So the first-IA target should not yet be reduced to only the mixed residual

R(T)ÔłłF2[Cd].

The safer corrected target is(¤Áb,R(T))ÔłłF2ÔŐĽF2[Cd],with ¤ÁS=0 only after fixing the usual SÔćö[¤ľ] marking. If a future turn proves that the b-line is globally normalized by the class [5], then ¤Áb can also be removed. Until then, Turn 73 over-discarded one universal square coordinate.The primitive detector from Turn 72 still applies to the mixed part. For every odd primitive anti-cyclotomic order c,╬öc(T)=ÔäôÔłúcÔłĆ(1+T c/Ôäô)detects the primitive c-component:primitive┬ác-part┬áof┬áRc=0Ôč║╬öc(T)Rc (T)=0in┬áF2[Cc].But the corrected first arithmetic tests should include the extra check ¤Áb=0, unless the bÔłĘ=[5] normalization is first established.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I found a flaw in Turn 73ÔÇÖs square-coordinate elimination: the class [Ôłĺ1] corresponds to the candidateÔÇÖs C2 -torsion regular norm line, not to the free b-line. Therefore fixedness of [Ôłĺ1]ÔłłL├Ś/L├Ś4 does not prove ¤Áb=0. The correct likely normalization is bÔłĘ=[5], SÔłĘ=[¤ľ], but that uniform identification still needs proof.

GAP: The presentation is still not proved. The remaining first-IA arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction should be treated as (¤Áb,R(T)), not just R(T), unless the b-line is rigorously identified with the fixed order-4 base-unit class [5]. After that, the mixed anti-cyclotomic residuals Rc (T) still need to be computed or killed by a dyadic formation theorem.

NEXT: Prove the Kummer-basis normalization bÔłĘ=[5] for every finite tame L/Q2, using the base GQ2 (2) specialization and tame corestriction/normal-basis compatibility. Then compute the corrected residuals in order c=3,5,9,11, testing ¤Áb=0 and ╬öcRc=0.[BREAKTHROUGH]
